assertions.assertion: Research orchestration requires clear delegation of specialized analysis tasks to parallel research tracks

do: Delegate protocol analysis to dedicated track
Assign whale movement tracking to behavioral analysis track
Create separate track for smart contract events
dont: Attempt to perform all analysis types in a single job
Create sequential dependencies between parallel research tracks
Mix different analysis methodologies in one task

assertions.commentary: Effective research requires specialization. The root orchestrator should delegate distinct research domains to parallel tracks that can execute simultaneously, maximizing efficiency and depth of analysis.

assertions.assertion: Final synthesis must wait for all research tracks to complete before integration

do: Use dependencies array to enforce completion order
Create synthesis job that depends on all research track job definitions
Wait for comprehensive data before drawing conclusions
dont: Start synthesis before research tracks complete
Make conclusions based on partial data
Create synthesis job without proper dependency wiring

assertions.commentary: Premature synthesis leads to incomplete conclusions. The orchestrator must ensure all research tracks complete before creating the synthesis job, using the dependency system to enforce proper execution order.

assertions.assertion: Research coordination requires clear scope definition and timeframe specification

do: Specify exact time window (last 24 hours on Ethereum)
Define scope as mainnet Ethereum blockchain only
Set clear deliverable expectations (top 3 trade ideas)
dont: Allow scope creep to multiple chains without clear rationale

assertions.commentary: Research quality depends on clear boundaries. The orchestrator must define precise scope, timeframe, and deliverable expectations to ensure focused, actionable research outputs.
